---
title: "The Science Repository"
subtitle: "A reproducible consumer decision-making analysis in R"
date: today
toc: false
---

This website is the **transparent, click-through version of the whole project**:
the code, the data cleaning, the figures, the tables, and the reasoning — all
rendered from one git repository. It is built with [Quarto](https://quarto.org)
from the sources in `reports/webpage/`, and every number you see was produced by
the shared R engine in [`R/`](https://github.com/TheDataFlowCompany/the-science-repository/tree/main/R).

## The study in one paragraph

Three hundred (simulated) shoppers each saw a product framed by one of five
messages — a plain `control`, plus `scarcity`, `sustainability`, `social_proof`,
and a `combined` frame — and then reported their attitudes and a hypothetical
purchase decision. We ask three questions:

1. Does the **message frame** raise **purchase intention**?
2. Is that effect **mediated** by how much **value** people perceive?
3. Is it **moderated** by how **price-sensitive** they are?

## Read it in order

The analysis is split into three short pages, each one stage of the pipeline:

| Page | What it does |
| --- | --- |
| [1 · Data preparation](01-data-preparation.qmd) | Load the raw CSV, inspect the mess, clean it, and **save** the tidy dataset other pages read. |
| [2 · Descriptives](02-descriptives.qmd) | Summarise the sample and **look** at the patterns before modelling. |
| [3 · Analysis](03-analysis.qmd) | The main effect, the mediation, and the moderation models. |

The [About](about.qmd) page holds the reproducibility, data-protection, and
AI-use statements.

## How this site is built

Nothing here is copy-pasted. Each page calls the same functions from `R/` and
reads the same cleaned dataset:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/TheDataFlowCompany/the-science-repository
cd the-science-repository
R -e 'renv::restore()'        # install the exact package versions
quarto render reports/webpage # builds this site into renders/webpage/
```

That is the workshop's core idea: **the `R/` folder is the single source of
truth for the numbers; this website and the journal manuscript are just two ways
of presenting them.** See the [project README](https://github.com/TheDataFlowCompany/the-science-repository#readme)
for the full folder map.
